name: Ada
known_facts: Field commander / scapegoat.
Listed KIA before final blast confirmation.
One of four Cape Wipeout survivors (with Tallai/Talla, Q, and Quartz).
Named in the refined Nago plate's in-world Honor & Origin line as one of those who carried Nago.
Full credited name and role per the Friedmandostorp rescue animatic: Adanna "Ada" Venter, credited Care Shooter.

Per `UMADA_CANON_INDEX.md` (2026-06-25): full_name given as "Adanna Venter" (no middle initial) — see open_question below for how this relates to the torn military record's "Adanna K. [Venter]" / "Kassey."
Per `UMADA_CANON_INDEX.md` (2026-06-25): described as "the first total cybernetic reconstruction case" — a new specific framing of her already-locked survival/reconstruction.
Per `UMADA_CANON_INDEX.md` (2026-06-25): KC is Ada's partner — see the KC entry below and Open Question #30 for survivor-status nuance.

Per `UMADA_CANON_INDEX.md` and the Book One canon package (both 2026-06-25): in the Council Era, Ada refuses to become the time traveler and instead chooses the Reporter, who carries the Omoluabi device to witness history.

EMERGING, sourced to a single 2026-06-27 fragment (`13_fragments/2026-06-27_studio_pipeline_package.md`), pending corroboration: visual/style descriptor — "elegant strong features, Iman-like presence, expressive face, grounded survivor energy, no glamour pose." This is the visual-design layer of the still-missing Character Dossier, not a complete biographical dossier.
open_question: The animatic burns the name "Kassey" across a torn military record for an "Adanna K. [Venter]" (stamped CLEARANCE DENIED, KIA, tagged Chimera Survivor / Scientist / Empath) in the same panel as Ada's introduction. Whether Kassey is the same person as Ada under a second name, a relative, or someone else is not stated — see 01_canon/VISUAL_CANON_REGISTRY.md and 01_canon/OPEN_QUESTIONS.md (#24).
dossier_status: AWAITING FRAGMENT (biography); visual descriptor EMERGING per 2026-06-27 fragment

name: KC
status: LOCKED (role, per UMADA_CANON_INDEX.md, 2026-06-25) / AWAITING FRAGMENT (full dossier)
known_facts: Ada's partner — male, human, Cape-operation operative.
Accepts limited restorative cybernetics for mobility; refuses full cybernetic restructuring, in explicit ethical contrast to Ada's total reconstruction.
Insists technology serve personhood and choice.
Present at the Cape Operation, meant to meet Ada at the Friedmandostorp bread stand before proceeding to the Cape laboratory.
At the moment of Cape Wipeout, KC is described as "separated" — not confirmed killed (Book One canon package, book/timeline.md).
Dies before/within living memory of the Council Era — after Tallai, per the Book One timeline's ordering of the Estate-of-Memory years.
open_question: KC is not added to the locked four-person Cape Wipeout survivor sentence (Ada, Tallai, Q, Quartz). Whether KC should eventually be treated as a fifth named survivor, or remains categorically distinct from that survivor unit, is Open Question #30. Whether "Kasey" (used informally by Kemi in chat) is a safe expansion of KC's name or risks the Canon Index's explicitly warned-against "Casey" spelling is Open Question #29.
source: UMADA_CANON_INDEX.md (2026-06-25); corroborated by the Book One canon package's book/timeline.md

name: Tallai / Talla
status: LOCKED (role) / OPEN QUESTION (preferred single spelling) / AWAITING FRAGMENT (full dossier)
known_facts: Chimera care specialist.
Known at the bread stop in Friedmandostorp before the blast.
Explores the Garden (Era 4).
One of four Cape Wipeout survivors.
Named in the refined Nago plate's in-world Honor & Origin line as one of those who carried Nago.

Per `UMADA_CANON_INDEX.md` (2026-06-25): the first being to discover/rescue Ada after Cape Wipeout, later Steward of Ada's Estate of Memory and caretaker of the sanctuary network and memory practices; chooses stewardship rather than ownership; death profoundly shapes Ada's grief and the Estate of Memory.

EMERGING, sourced to a single 2026-06-27 fragment (`13_fragments/2026-06-27_studio_pipeline_package.md`), pending corroboration: visual/style descriptor — "tall, veiled, patient, observant," "lab-coat or practical cloth layers," "loves morning bread." This is the visual-design layer of the still-missing Character Dossier, not a complete biographical dossier.
open_question: Both 'Tallai' and 'Talla' are attested across source documents. Whether this is a nickname/full-name pair or a spelling that should converge on one form is open — see 01_canon/OPEN_QUESTIONS.md. Separately, whether Tallai belongs to the Lion or Jaguar chimera lineage (per `UMADA_CANON_INDEX.md`, 2026-06-25) is not stated — see Open Question #31.
dossier_status: AWAITING FRAGMENT (biography); visual descriptor EMERGING per 2026-06-27 fragment

One of four Cape Wipeout survivors.
Not named in the refined Nago plate's in-world Honor & Origin line, which credits Tallai, Ada, Q, and "the people" — see open question in 01_canon/OPEN_QUESTIONS.md.

name: The Reporter / Time Traveler
status: LOCKED (role, per UMADA_CANON_INDEX.md, 2026-06-25) / AWAITING FRAGMENT (full dossier)
known_facts: AI-enhanced hybrid human-cybernetic; active during the opening frame.
Observer, time traveler, narrator, and carrier of the Omoluabi device.
Chosen by Ada — a distinct character from Ada, not Ada herself; Ada refuses to become the time traveler.
Sent to witness, not change, history.
Voice is precise, reflective, historically corrective.
source: UMADA_CANON_INDEX.md (2026-06-25); corroborated by the Book One canon package's book/timeline.md
